groups.title: Ferry to Shetland Islands
groups.desc:
Questions 1-10
Complete the notes below.
Write ONE WORD AND/OR A NUMBER for each answer.
groups.collect: Ferry to Shetland Islands Name of ferry company: $22227$ Ferries Ferries depart seven times per $22228$ in summer Cost for four people with car: a little less than £$22229$ Cancellation policy: receive a $22230$ if cancelled a month in advance Advice Cabins: book one with a $22231$; luxury cabins have a TV Bring snacks and $22232$ for the children A $22233$ is required for the dog kennels Try to see $22234$ in the morning If time, visit $22235$ Castle The $22236$ restaurant in a nearby village is recommended

questions.analysis: 题干询问渡轮公司的名称，原文中Paul明确回答：‘There's only one – it's called Northern Ferries.’ 其中‘Northern’是公司名称的首词，且表格中已给出‘Ferries’，因此只需填写‘Northern’。该信息直接、无干扰，符合专有名词填空的要求。
题目要求填写夏季每周渡轮发船次数对应的时间单位。原文提到‘there's only one ferry leaving every evening in summer anyway, seven days a week’，其中‘seven days a week’与题干‘seven times per ___’形成同义替换，因此答案为‘week’。此处需注意频率表达的转换逻辑。
题干问四人带车的票价，原文Paul说‘four people and a car worked out at just under £250’，‘just under’与题干中的‘a little less than’构成同义替换，因此答案为‘250’或‘two hundred and fifty’。虽然Tammy提到预期£400，但那是干扰信息，正确答案以Paul的实际支付为准。
题干涉及取消政策，原文明确指出‘I don't think it's their policy to give refunds – just a voucher’，并说明需提前一个月取消才能获得该凭证。‘voucher’（代金券）直接对应空格内容，且是唯一提及的补偿形式，排除了‘refund’等干扰项。
题干建议预订带某种特征的舱房，原文Paul提到‘We booked too late to get a cabin with a window’，并认为带窗舱房‘much nicer’，因此推荐预订的是‘window’舱。此处‘window’作为关键区分特征，与后文‘inner cabins’形成对比，答案明确。
题干要求填写为孩子携带的物品，与‘snacks’并列。原文中Tammy问及船上Wi-Fi，Paul回答‘Not really. So it's best to bring some books for them.’ 因此‘books’是因网络不佳而建议携带的替代娱乐物品，与‘snacks’共同构成对孩子旅途的准备建议。
题干问狗舍所需物品，原文Paul说‘The kennels on board are OK – they're quite big – you just need to provide a blanket.’ 其中‘provide a blanket’直接对应‘a ___ is required’，因此答案为‘blanket’。这是唯一提及的必需品，无其他干扰选项。
题干要求填写早上尝试看到的动物，原文Paul提到孩子们‘in the morning keeping a lookout for dolphins – we saw loads.’ ‘keeping a lookout for dolphins’与题干‘Try to see ___’形成直接对应，因此答案为‘dolphins’。该信息出现在描述孩子体验的语境中，逻辑清晰。
题干要求填写城堡名称，原文Paul直接回答‘Drum Castle’，且随后Tammy确认拼写‘Is that spelt like the instrument?’，进一步强调该专有名词的重要性。表格中已给出‘Castle’，因此只需填写‘Drum’。这是唯一提及的景点名称，无其他干扰。
题干询问附近村庄推荐的餐厅类型，原文Paul说‘found an Italian one in a village nearby’，其中‘Italian one’指代‘Italian restaurant’。题干中‘The ___ restaurant’要求填写菜系或国籍形容词，因此‘Italian’准确对应原文的指代，且是唯一提到的餐厅类型。

transcript.en: Part 1
You will hear a woman asking a friend for advice on travelling by ferry to an island in Scotland.
transcript.cn: 你将听到一位女士向朋友咨询乘渡轮前往苏格兰某岛的建议。

transcript.en: First, you have some time to look at questions 1 to 4.
transcript.cn: 首先，你有一些时间查看第1至第4题。

transcript.en: Now listen carefully and answer questions 1 to 4.
transcript.cn: 现在请仔细听，并回答第1至第4题。

transcript.en: TAMMY: You know when you went to the Shetland Islands last year, Paul? Did you go by ferry or did you fly?
transcript.cn: 塔米：保罗，你知道你去年去谢德兰群岛吗？你是坐渡轮去的还是坐飞机去的？

transcript.en: PAUL: We went by ferry, Tammy. I prefer driving to flying – the journey feels like part of the holiday.
transcript.cn: 保罗：我们坐渡轮去的，塔米。我更喜欢开车而不是坐飞机——旅途感觉像是假期的一部分。

transcript.en: TAMMY: Mmm. Which ferry company did you use?
transcript.cn: 塔米：嗯，你们用的是哪家渡轮公司？

transcript.en: PAUL: There's only one – it's called Northern Ferries. The ferries all leave from Aberdeen.
transcript.cn: 保罗：只有一家——叫北方渡轮公司，渡轮都从阿伯丁出发。

transcript.en: TAMMY: How frequent are they?
transcript.cn: 塔米：班次频繁吗？

transcript.en: PAUL: The service is pretty limited – there's only one ferry leaving every evening in summer anyway, seven days a week – I'm not sure about the winter months. They may only run on four or five days then.
transcript.cn: 保罗：服务相当有限——夏天每天晚上只有一班渡轮，每周七天——我不确定冬季的情况，那时可能只有四五天有班次。

transcript.en: TAMMY: OK. So it's an overnight trip. I quite like that idea. Leaving at night and waking up as you arrive on the island. Can you remember how much you paid for your tickets?
transcript.cn: 塔米：好的，所以是一次过夜的旅行。我挺喜欢这个想法的，晚上出发，醒来时就到达岛上了。你记得你们的票价是多少吗？

transcript.en: PAUL: They were really cheap and four people and a car worked out at just under £250.
transcript.cn: 保罗：真的很便宜，四个人加一辆车合计不到250英镑。

transcript.en: TAMMY: Really? I was expecting it to be more like £400 during the peak season.
transcript.cn: 塔米：真的？我以为旺季会在400英镑左右。

transcript.en: PAUL: So was I. It's great value. It's a good idea to book in advance because I think they get booked up quite quickly – especially during the school holidays.
transcript.cn: 保罗：我也是这么以为的，真的很划算。提前预订是个好主意，因为我认为他们的票会很快订完——尤其是在学校假期期间。

transcript.en: TAMMY: Yes, I suppose so. I'm just not 100% sure of our plans yet. What if I had to cancel?
transcript.cn: 塔米：是的，我想是的。我只是还不100%确定我们的计划，如果我必须取消怎么办？

transcript.en: PAUL: That could be a problem. I don't think it's their policy to give refunds – just a voucher – which you can use at a later date. But you have to cancel a month in advance to get that.
transcript.cn: 保罗：那可能是个问题。我不认为他们的政策是退款——只是一张代金券——您可以在以后使用。但您必须提前一个月取消才能获得代金券。

transcript.en: TAMMY: Right. Well we need to make up our minds quickly then.
transcript.cn: 塔米：好的，那我们需要尽快做决定了。

transcript.en: Before you hear the rest of the conversation, you have some time to look at questions 5 to 10.
transcript.cn: 在你听剩余对话之前，你有一些时间查看第5至第10题。

transcript.en: Now listen and answer questions 5 to 10.
transcript.cn: 现在请听录音，并回答第5至第10题。

transcript.en: PAUL: You'd want to book a cabin too. We booked too late to get a cabin with a window. They're more expensive but much nicer than the inner cabins. You don't have to book a cabin at all but I think it's worth paying for. They also have luxury cabins, which are only for two people and have a TV – but I wouldn't bother with those.
transcript.cn: 保罗：您也会想预订一个船舱的。我们预订得太晚了，没有订到有窗户的船舱。它们更贵，但比内舱好多了。您完全不必预订船舱，但我认为值得付费预订。他们还有豪华船舱，只供两人使用并配有电视——但我不会麻烦去订那个。

transcript.en: TAMMY: No, I agree.
transcript.cn: 塔米：不，我同意。

transcript.en: PAUL: The only other thing I can think of is to make sure you bring snacks for the kids. The selection on board is quite limited and not that healthy either.
transcript.cn: 保罗：我能想到的唯一一件事是确保为孩子们带零食，船上的选择相当有限，而且也不那么健康。

transcript.en: TAMMY: Mmm. What about wifi on board? Is that any good?
transcript.cn: 塔米：嗯，船上的WiFi怎么样？好用吗？

transcript.en: PAUL: Not really. So it's best to bring some books for them.
transcript.cn: 保罗：不太好，所以最好给他们带些书。

transcript.en: TAMMY: OK. We may need to bring the dog if I can't get anyone to look after him.
transcript.cn: 塔米：好的，如果我找不到人照看狗的话，我们可能需要带上它。

transcript.en: PAUL: We brought ours and it was fine. The kennels on board are OK – they're quite big – you just need to provide a blanket.
transcript.cn: 保罗：我们带了我们的狗，一切都很好。船上的狗舍还不错——相当大——您只需要提供一条毯子。

transcript.en: TAMMY: Uhuh. Sounds good.
transcript.cn: 塔米：嗯，听起来不错。

transcript.en: PAUL: It was all very easy really – and it was quite an adventure for the kids. They loved being on the sea at night and in the morning keeping a lookout for dolphins – we saw loads.
transcript.cn: 保罗：一切都非常简单——对孩子们来说是一次相当有趣的冒险。他们喜欢在夜晚航行，早上寻找海豚——我们看到了很多。

transcript.en: TAMMY: Oh, the kids would love that!
transcript.cn: 塔米：哦，孩子们一定会喜欢的！

transcript.en: PAUL: One other thing. We arrived in Aberdeen hours before the ferry was due to leave so we decided to go somewhere else rather than hang around at the port for so long.
transcript.cn: 保罗：还有一件事，我们在渡轮预定出发前几个小时就到了阿伯丁，所以我们决定去别处，而不是在港口等那么长时间。

transcript.en: TAMMY: Where did you go?
transcript.cn: 塔米：你们去哪里了？

transcript.en: PAUL: Drum Castle.
transcript.cn: 保罗：德拉姆城堡。

transcript.en: TAMMY: I've never heard of it. Is that spelt like the instrument?
transcript.cn: 塔米：我从未听说过它，拼写和乐器一样吗？

transcript.en: PAUL: Yeah. It's really worth visiting. It's got an impressive tower and beautiful gardens and ancient woodland.
transcript.cn: 保罗：是的，真的值得参观，有令人印象深刻的塔楼、美丽的花园和古老的林地。

transcript.en: TAMMY: Sounds lovely. Does it have a restaurant?
transcript.cn: 塔米：听起来很美，有餐厅吗？

transcript.en: PAUL: It's only got a coffee shop – no restaurant. We looked up restaurants in the area and found an Italian one in a village nearby. I can check the name of it for you if you're interested.
transcript.cn: 保罗：只有咖啡馆——没有餐厅。我们查了该地区的餐厅，在附近的一个村子里找到了一家意大利餐厅，如果您感兴趣，我可以为您查一下它的名字。

transcript.en: TAMMY: Oh thanks Paul that would be ….
transcript.cn: 塔米：哦，谢谢你保罗，那就……

groups.title: Which TWO explanations for the popularity of street food are given?
groups.desc:
Questions 11 and 12
Choose TWO letters, A-E.

collect_option.title: Which TWO explanations for the popularity of street food are given?

questions.analysis: 题目要求选出街边食品受欢迎的两个原因。原文中提到，人们喜欢尝试‘无法在别处获得的东西’（对应E选项‘unusual food’），也喜欢看到食物‘在他们面前现做，而不是直接从冷冻柜取出’（对应C选项‘freshly made’）。这两个要点分别出现在第10句和第11句，是说话人明确指出的受欢迎原因，而其他选项如低价、便利性或本地采购并未被提及。

此题与第11题为同一组多选题，正确答案同样是C和E。听力原文强调街边食品的独特性（‘something they can't get anywhere else’）和现场制作的新鲜感（‘prepared in front of them’），分别对应‘unusual food’和‘freshly made’。虽然提到了快餐便宜且普遍，但这是作为对比，并非街边食品受欢迎的正面原因，因此A和D不成立。

groups.title: Which TWO places are recommended for new street food businesses?
groups.desc:
Questions 13 and 14
Choose TWO letters, A-E.

collect_option.title: Which TWO places are recommended for new street food businesses?

questions.analysis: 题目询问哪两个地点适合新开的街边食品生意。原文明确推荐了‘food markets’（第14句：‘great because customers are really interested in food’）和‘local park’（第15句：‘fantastic... with lots of customers passing by’），分别对应B和E选项。虽然也提到婚礼和派对，但这些是‘established’之后才可能接到的业务，并非对新手的推荐；音乐节则因成本高、竞争大被排除。

本题与第13题同属一组，正确答案仍为B（food markets）和E（parks）。听力中说话人用‘great’和‘fantastic’积极评价这两个地点，强调其顾客流量大且氛围轻松。而音乐节被指出‘cost... huge’和‘a lot of competition’，婚礼和派对则属于后期业务，不适合初学者。因此只有B和E符合‘recommended for new businesses’的要求。

groups.desc:
Questions 15-16
Choose the correct letter, A, B or C.

questions.title: What does the speaker say about getting equipment for a street food business?

options.title: A
options.content: High quality equipment is a good investment.

options.title: B
options.content: It is best to buy second-hand equipment.

questions.analysis: 题目问关于设备采购的建议。原文第20句明确说‘I'd try to get things like hobs and fridges second hand if you can’，直接支持B选项‘最好购买二手设备’。虽然提到日后可换更高质量的设备，但并未说一开始就应投资高端设备（排除A）；相反，第21句指出租赁‘you'll end up spending more money’，说明C选项错误。

questions.title: What advice is given about creating a product for a street food business?

options.title: B
options.content: It is important to have an original product.

options.title: C
options.content: The presentation is an important factor.

questions.analysis: 题目询问关于产品开发的建议。原文第24句强调食物‘要容易吃且看起来吸引人，否则顾客不会回头’（‘easy to eat and look attractive’），这直接对应C选项‘presentation is an important factor’。虽然提到过敏原，但未强调提供食材信息（A）；也未讨论产品是否原创（B），因此C是唯一有原文支撑的选项。

groups.title: Problems
groups.desc:
Questions 17-20
What problem did the owners of each of the following street food businesses experience?
Choose FOUR answers from the box and write the correct letter, A-F, next to Questions 17-20.

list.title: B
list.content: The meals took a long time to prepare.

list.title: C
list.content: They had no money for marketing.

list.title: D
list.content: It was difficult to get a permit to sell food.

list.title: E
list.content: A competitor was selling similar food in their area.

questions.title: Thai Basil
questions.analysis: Thai Basil的问题在于经营者同时兼顾餐厅全职工作和周末街边生意，导致‘work-life balance was non-existent’且‘working til midnight... then took their food truck on days off’，这明显对应F选项‘worked very long hours’。其他选项如原料贵、许可难等均未涉及，因此F是唯一符合原文描述的答案。

questions.title: Basque
questions.analysis: Basque的问题是鱼类价格过高（‘price of fish... was so high’），而鱼又是其部分菜品的关键原料，导致难以盈利，最终不得不减少鱼类菜品。这直接对应A选项‘Some ingredients were too expensive’。文中未提准备时间长、营销资金或竞争对手等问题，因此其他选项均可排除。

questions.title: Lou's kitchen
questions.analysis: Lou's Kitchen的问题是菜品过于复杂，导致顾客排队超过15分钟并失去耐心（‘dishes were quite complicated... queue for more than 15 minutes’），这说明餐品准备耗时过长，对应B选项‘The meals took a long time to prepare’。原文强调‘serve quickly and efficiently’的重要性，进一步印证B为正确答案，其他选项均无依据。

questions.title: Chip Chop
questions.analysis: Chip Chop的问题是他们在海滩附近找到理想地点后，才发现需要特殊许可证（‘special licence’），而该许可在市集或音乐节并不需要，申请过程复杂最终放弃。这对应D选项‘It was difficult to get a permit to sell food’。‘licence’在此语境下即指经营许可（permit），其他选项如原料成本、竞争或营销问题均未提及。

transcript.en: PART 2
You will hear a man giving a talk to people who are interested in starting their own street food business.
transcript.cn: 你将听到一名男子给有意开始自己街头食品生意的人做演讲。

transcript.en: First, you have some time to look at questions 11 to 14.
transcript.cn: 首先，你有一些时间查看第11至第14题。

transcript.en: Now listen carefully and answer questions 11 to 14.
transcript.cn: 现在请仔细听，并回答第11至第14题。

transcript.en: Good evening everyone. My name's Jon and I run Veg Out, a street food business selling vegan food.
transcript.cn: 大家晚上好，我叫乔恩，我经营一家名为Veg Out的街头食品企业，专门出售纯素食品。

transcript.en: Since 2012 I've been travelling all around the country cooking vegan food in my converted van and selling it at all kinds of outdoor events. I'm here to give you some advice based on my experience.
transcript.cn: 自2012年以来，我一直在全国各地旅行，在我改装的厢式货车里烹制纯素食品，并在各种户外活动中出售。我在这里根据我的经验给你们一些建议。

transcript.en: The good news is that there's never been a better time to start your own street food business. Street food continues to grow in popularity. I think there are a couple of reasons for this.
transcript.cn: 好消息是，现在是开创自己的街头食品业务的最佳时机。街头食品持续受到欢迎，我认为有几个原因。

transcript.en: The first is that street food is a reaction against fast food. Street food ranges from high quality burgers to vegan curries and everything in between.
transcript.cn: 第一个是街头食品是对快餐的一种反应。街头食品从高质量汉堡到纯素咖喱，涵盖各种各样的食物。

transcript.en: But while fast food is cheap and easy to find, it's not particularly good for you. It's also the same everywhere.
transcript.cn: 但虽然快餐便宜且容易找到，对您不特别有益，而且到处都一样。

transcript.en: What you get with street food on the other hand is something different. People like the idea of trying something they can't get anywhere else.
transcript.cn: 另一方面，街头食品给您带来的是不同的东西。

transcript.en: They also like seeing food prepared in front of them and which hasn't come straight out of a freezer.
transcript.cn: 人们喜欢尝试其他地方找不到的东西，也喜欢看到食物在他们面前准备，而不是直接从冰柜里取出来的。

transcript.en: You need to think about the best place to sell your street food.
transcript.cn: 您需要考虑出售街头食品的最佳地点。

transcript.en: People always think music festivals are an obvious place to start but the cost of renting a space can be huge. And there's always a lot of competition.
transcript.cn: 人们总是认为音乐节是一个明显的起点，但租用场地的费用可能很高，而且竞争总是很激烈。

transcript.en: Food markets, on the other hand, are great because customers are always really interested in food and give great feedback.
transcript.cn: 另一方面，食品市场非常棒，因为顾客总是对食物非常感兴趣，并给出很好的反馈。

transcript.en: And if you can get a spot in your local park – fantastic. Usually very relaxed but with lots of customers passing by.
transcript.cn: 如果您能在当地公园找到一个摊位——太棒了，通常非常轻松，但有很多顾客经过。

transcript.en: Once you get established you'll start getting asked to do parties – which can be really challenging but lots of fun.
transcript.cn: 一旦您站稳脚跟，您将开始被邀请举办派对——这可能非常具有挑战性，但也很有趣。

transcript.en: Having street food at weddings has become quite fashionable too – but you need to really know what you're doing as everything needs to be perfect.
transcript.cn: 在婚礼上提供街头食品也变得相当时髦——但您需要真正知道自己在做什么，因为一切都需要完美。

transcript.en: Before you hear the rest of the talk, you have some time to look at questions 15 to 20.
transcript.cn: 在你听演讲剩余部分之前，你有一些时间查看第15至第20题。

transcript.en: Now listen and answer questions 15 to 20.
transcript.cn: 现在请听录音，并回答第15至第20题。

transcript.en: Setting up a street food business costs a lot less than opening a restaurant or café but you'll have to buy some basic equipment. I'd try to get things like hobs and fridges second hand if you can.
transcript.cn: 开设街头食品业务的成本远低于开设餐厅或咖啡馆，但您需要购买一些基本设备。如果可以的话，我会尝试买二手的炉灶和冰箱。

transcript.en: You can replace them with better quality stuff if your business takes off. Renting is another option but you'll end up spending more money rather than saving it.
transcript.cn: 如果您的业务起步，您可以用质量更好的设备替换它们。租赁是另一种选择，但您最终会花更多的钱而不是节省。

transcript.en: You've probably got a good idea about the food you're planning to sell.
transcript.cn: 您可能对您计划出售的食物有了很好的想法。

transcript.en: I expect you've done some research to find out if anyone else is selling a similar product. And you'll have thought about any possible allergies to nuts or eggs etc.
transcript.cn: 我相信您已经做了一些研究，以了解是否有其他人在出售类似的产品，您也会考虑过对坚果或鸡蛋等的可能过敏。

transcript.en: But there's one thing people don't always think about and that's how you're going to serve it. On a plate? In a bag? Will you provide a fork? Will it all be easily recyclable or reusable? It's got to be easy to eat and look attractive or customers won't come back.
transcript.cn: 但有一件事人们并不总是想到，那就是您打算如何供应食物。用盘子？用袋子？您会提供叉子吗？这些都容易回收或重复使用吗？必须易于食用且看起来有吸引力，否则顾客不会再来。

transcript.en: Once you get started, you should be prepared for things to go wrong. Every business faces problems and here are a few examples from street food businesses that I know.
transcript.cn: 一旦您开始，您应该做好事情出错的准备。每个企业都面临问题，以下是我认识的一些街头食品企业的例子。

transcript.en: My friends who run Thai Basil started by juggling their street food business with their day jobs in a restaurant.
transcript.cn: 我经营泰国罗勒的朋友们从同时兼顾街头食品业务和餐厅的日常工作开始。

transcript.en: Their work-life balance was non-existent as they were working til midnight in the restaurant all week and then took their food truck to markets on their days off.
transcript.cn: 他们几乎没有工作与生活的平衡，因为他们整个星期都在餐厅工作到午夜，然后在休息日把食品卡车开到市场。

transcript.en: The owners of Basque found it was hard to make a profit because the price of fish – essential for some of their dishes – was so high.
transcript.cn: 巴斯克的老板们发现很难盈利，因为鱼的价格——他们某些菜肴必不可少的食材——太高了。

transcript.en: And it was hard to charge customers a lot more for those dishes. So they had to stop focussing on fish dishes and include more vegetarian food.
transcript.cn: 而且很难向顾客收取更多的钱来补偿这些菜肴，所以他们不得不停止专注于鱼类菜肴，转而包含更多素食食品。

transcript.en: The owners of Lou's kitchen were making salads to order from their van and some of their dishes were quite complicated.
transcript.cn: 露的厨房的老板在他们的货车里按订单制作沙拉，他们的一些菜肴相当复杂。

transcript.en: At one of their first events they ended up with people standing in a long queue for more than 15 minutes – and many of them lost patience. So make sure whatever you offer can be served quickly and efficiently.
transcript.cn: 在他们最初的一次活动中，结果人们排着长队等了超过15分钟——许多人失去了耐心。所以确保您提供的任何东西都能快速高效地供应。

transcript.en: The owners of Chip Chop had found a perfect venue near a beach where there weren't any other street food trucks.
transcript.cn: 炸薯条店的老板找到了一个完美的海滩附近场地，那里没有其他街头食品车。

transcript.en: But what they hadn't realised was that they'd need a special licence – which individual businesses don't need at markets or festivals. It was a complicated process and in the end they gave up.
transcript.cn: 但他们没有意识到的是，他们需要一个特殊的许可证——这是个人企业在市场或节日上不需要的。这是一个复杂的过程，最终他们放弃了。

transcript.en: So I hope that's given you a flavour of ….
transcript.cn: 所以我希望这让您初步了解了……

groups.title: Which TWO points do the speakers make about the terms 'ethical' and 'sustainable' fashion?
groups.desc:
Questions 21 and 22
Choose TWO letters, A-E.

list.title: B
list.content: People think they mean the same thing.

list.title: E
list.content: Companies should avoid using them on clothing labels.

collect_option.title: Which TWO points do the speakers make about the terms 'ethical' and 'sustainable' fashion?

questions.analysis: 题目要求选出关于‘ethical’和‘sustainable’时尚的两个正确观点。原文中Maddy指出人们常将这两个术语互换使用（interchangeably），对应选项B‘人们认为它们意思相同’；同时Maddy提到公司描述产品时故意模糊（deliberately vague），对应选项D‘这些术语常被不精确地使用’。其他选项在文中未被提及或与说话人观点不符。
本题与第21题为同一道双选题，共享定位信息。对话中Maddy明确表示大众混淆‘ethical’与‘sustainable’的概念，并批评企业用词含糊。因此选项B（人们认为两词同义）和D（术语使用不精确）均被直接支持，符合题干要求选出的两个观点。

groups.title: Which TWO claims about wool production do the speakers disagree with?
groups.desc:
Questions 23 and 24
Choose TWO letters, A-E.

list.title: E
list.content: Sheep do less environmental damage than other livestock.

collect_option.title: Which TWO claims about wool production do the speakers disagree with?

questions.analysis: 题目问的是两位说话人不同意的关于羊毛生产的两项说法。Ryan质疑‘羊毛生产几乎不用化学品’的说法，指出养羊过程中使用了大量杀菌剂和杀虫剂；Maddy则表示找不到证据证明‘羊比牛对环境危害更小’。这两点分别对应选项D和E，是他们明确反驳的内容。
此题与第23题共用同一组选项和定位句。Ryan和Maddy分别对‘羊毛生产少用化学品’（D）和‘羊比其他牲畜对环境破坏小’（E）提出质疑，表明他们不认同这两个说法。因此D和E是两人共同反对的观点，符合题目要求。

groups.title: Comments
groups.desc:
Questions 25-30
What comment do the speakers make about each of the following semi-synthetic fabrics?
Choose SIX answers from the box and write the correct letter, A-H, next to Questions 25-30.

list.title: A
list.content: The production process is fuel efficient.

list.title: B
list.content: It is the least sustainable of alternative fabrics.

list.title: E
list.content: It is not durable in the long-term.

list.title: F
list.content: It needs to be produced in a certain way to be sustainable.

list.title: G
list.content: Chemicals required for production can be reused.

list.title: H
list.content: This is from a wholly sustainable source.

questions.title: Lyocell
questions.analysis: 关于Lyocell，Ryan提到其制造过程中超过99%的溶解剂（dissolving agents）会被重复使用。这直接对应选项G‘生产所需的化学品可被重复利用’。该信息紧接在介绍Lyocell之后，无其他干扰项，因此G是唯一准确匹配的答案。

questions.title: Cupro
questions.analysis: 谈到Cupro时，Ryan指出其制造过程造成大量污染，Maddy也回应说它与其他可持续面料相比并不占优。这说明两人认为Cupro的可持续性较差，对应选项B‘它是替代面料中最不可持续的’。虽然原文未用‘least’一词，但通过比较语气可合理推断出此结论。

questions.title: Bamboo
questions.analysis: 关于Bamboo，Ryan强调只有有机竹子才是真正可持续的，而Maddy补充说大部分竹纤维的制造过程化学强度很高。这表明Bamboo必须以特定方式（即有机方式）生产才可持续，对应选项F。其他选项如耐久性或成本在文中未被讨论。

questions.title: EcoVero
questions.analysis: Maddy提到EcoVero的制造过程排放减少50%，能耗仅为传统面料的一半，这直接对应选项A‘生产过程燃料高效’。虽然原文用‘energy’而非‘fuel’，但在环保语境下二者可视为同义替换，且该描述明确指向能源效率高这一特点。

questions.title: Cork
questions.analysis: Maddy称软木（cork）是‘唯一从根本上可持续的面料’，因其来源可再生、产品可回收且可生物降解；Ryan补充说采收过程对树木有益，毫无缺点。这些描述完全契合选项H‘它来自一个完全可持续的来源’，是六个选项中最全面匹配的答案。

questions.title: Hemp
questions.analysis: Ryan提到 hemp 制成的衣物具有防晒和抗菌功能，Maddy虽指出其种植困难但未否定这些特性。这些额外的健康益处直接对应选项D。其他选项如耐久性、成本或可持续性虽有间接涉及，但‘health benefits’是唯一被明确陈述的特点。

transcript.en: PART 3
You will hear part of a discussion between two textile students about their research into sustainable fashion.
transcript.cn: 你将听到两名纺织专业学生讨论他们对可持续时尚的研究。

transcript.en: First, you have some time to look at questions 21 to 24.
transcript.cn: 首先，你有一些时间查看第21至第24题。

transcript.en: Now listen carefully and answer questions 21 to 24.
transcript.cn: 现在请仔细听，并回答第21至第24题。

transcript.en: MADDY: Shall we go through our research for our sustainable fashion project?
transcript.cn: 麦迪：我们来回顾一下我们可持续时尚项目的研究吧？

transcript.en: RYAN: Good idea. I think I've done enough reading now.
transcript.cn: 瑞恩：好主意，我想我现在已经读了足够多了。

transcript.en: MADDY: Me too. I've learnt such a lot about what sustainability actually means.
transcript.cn: 麦迪：我也是，我学到了很多关于可持续性的真正含义。

transcript.en: RYAN: Mmm, same for me with ethical fashion. I didn't really appreciate the difference between that and sustainable fashion before doing this research.
transcript.cn: 瑞恩：嗯，我对道德时尚的了解也一样。在做这项研究之前，我并没有真正理解道德时尚与可持续时尚之间的区别。

transcript.en: MADDY: I know – most people use these terms interchangeably – but in fact the difference is quite distinct when you think about it. Sustainable relates to the environment and ethical relates more to the way workers or animals are treated.
transcript.cn: 麦迪：我知道——大多数人互换使用这些术语——但实际上，当您仔细想想时，这两者的区别是相当明显的。可持续性与环境有关，而道德性则更多涉及对待工人或动物的方式。

transcript.en: RYAN: I totally understand why people get confused, though. There are so many other terms used – like 'eco-friendly', which is actually quite meaningless.
transcript.cn: 瑞恩：不过，我完全理解为什么人们会感到困惑。有很多其他术语被使用——比如「环保」，实际上这个词相当没有意义。

transcript.en: MADDY: And the way companies use these terms when describing their products doesn't help. They're often deliberately vague, I think, and don't provide enough information about how their products are made.
transcript.cn: 麦迪：而且公司在描述其产品时使用这些术语的方式也没有帮助。我认为他们往往是故意含糊其辞，没有提供足够的关于其产品如何制造的信息。

transcript.en: RYAN: Yes.
transcript.cn: 瑞恩：是的。

transcript.en: MADDY: It was interesting to read about the debates surrounding wool production and how ethical and sustainable that is. It's generally considered to be sustainable because it's a natural product.
transcript.cn: 麦迪：读到关于羊毛生产及其道德性和可持续性的争论很有趣。它通常被认为是可持续的，因为它是天然产品。

transcript.en: RYAN: And it also lasts a long time and can be recycled. All very positive. But I wasn't convinced by the argument that wool production is sustainable because it doesn't use many chemicals – what about all the fungicides and insecticides used in sheep farming?
transcript.cn: 瑞恩：而且它经久耐用，可以回收，都非常积极。但我不被羊毛生产因不使用太多化学品而可持续的论点所说服——那么养羊中使用的所有杀真菌剂和杀虫剂呢？

transcript.en: MADDY: Good point. And I couldn't find any evidence for the claim about sheep farming being better for the environment than cattle farming.
transcript.cn: 麦迪：好观点，我找不到任何证据支持养羊比养牛对环境更好的说法。

transcript.en: RYAN: No – they're both really bad. I read different reports about how unethical it is to even shear sheep. Some people say it's cruel but as long as the sheep are kept in good condition I can't see anything wrong with it.
transcript.cn: 瑞恩：不——它们都很糟糕。我读到不同的报告，说甚至剪羊毛都是不道德的。有些人说这是残忍的，但只要羊保持良好状态，我看不出有什么问题。

transcript.en: MADDY: Me neither.
transcript.cn: 麦迪：我也是。

transcript.en: Before you hear the rest of the discussion, you have some time to look at questions 25 to 30.
transcript.cn: 在你听剩余讨论之前，你有一些时间查看第25至第30题。

transcript.en: Now listen and answer questions 25 to 30.
transcript.cn: 现在请听录音，并回答第25至第30题。

transcript.en: RYAN: Shall we talk about some of the semi-synthetic new fabrics now?
transcript.cn: 瑞恩：我们现在来谈谈一些新的半合成织物吧？

transcript.en: MADDY: OK, let's do that.
transcript.cn: 麦迪：好的，我们来谈吧。

transcript.en: RYAN: Let's start with Lyocell, I've been reading about that.
transcript.cn: 瑞恩：我们从莱赛尔开始，我一直在阅读相关内容。

transcript.en: MADDY: Yeah, that's the one produced from the pulp of eucalyptus trees, isn't it?
transcript.cn: 麦迪：是的，那是由桉树浆生产的，不是吗？

transcript.en: RYAN: Yes, and what happens with that is really impressive. Over 99% of dissolving agents used in the manufacturing process are used again.
transcript.cn: 瑞恩：是的，其生产过程令人印象深刻，制造过程中使用的99%以上的溶剂会被再次使用。

transcript.en: MADDY: Yeah. Now, there are a few semi-synthetic fabrics that I'd never heard of. Like Cupro, for example.
transcript.cn: 麦迪：是的。现在，有一些我以前从未听说过的半合成织物，比如铜氨纤维。

transcript.en: RYAN: Made from byproducts of the cotton industry to create a kind of vegan silk. But I'm not sure how sustainable this really is as there are so many reports of pollution caused by the manufacturing process.
transcript.cn: 瑞恩：由棉花工业的副产品制成，创造出一种纯素丝绸。但我不确定这究竟有多可持续，因为有很多关于制造过程造成污染的报道。

transcript.en: MADDY: Mmm. It doesn't compare favourably with all the other sustainable fabrics we've looked at, no.
transcript.cn: 麦迪：嗯，与我们研究过的所有其他可持续织物相比，它并不占优势，不。

transcript.en: RYAN: Bamboo is one fabric we're all familiar with. But I didn't know that it was only organic bamboo that's truly sustainable.
transcript.cn: 瑞恩：竹子是我们都熟悉的一种织物。但我不知道只有有机竹子才是真正可持续的。

transcript.en: MADDY: Me neither. Apparently, the manufacturing process for a significant proportion of bamboo is chemically quite intensive – which obviously can be quite damaging.
transcript.cn: 麦迪：我也不知道。显然，相当大比例的竹子的制造过程在化学上相当密集——这显然可能造成相当大的损害。

transcript.en: RYAN: EcoVero is an example of a semi-synthetic fabric which is becoming really popular.
transcript.cn: 瑞恩：EcoVero是一种越来越受欢迎的半合成织物的例子。

transcript.en: MADDY: Probably because manufacturing causes 50 percent fewer emissions and takes up half as much energy as conventional fabrics. That saves production costs as well as being better for the environment.
transcript.cn: 麦迪：可能是因为制造过程产生的排放减少了50%，消耗的能量也只有传统织物的一半，这既节省了生产成本，对环境也更好。

transcript.en: RYAN: That's true. I think demand for cork will continue to grow. It works really well in vegan shoes and bags.
transcript.cn: 瑞恩：确实如此。我认为对软木的需求将继续增长，它在纯素鞋和包中效果非常好。

transcript.en: MADDY: Mmm and it's the only fabric that's fundamentally sustainable – the cork trees it comes from are renewable and the product itself is both recyclable and biodegradable – which is unique.
transcript.cn: 麦迪：嗯，它是唯一根本上可持续的织物——它来自的软木树是可再生的，产品本身既可回收又可生物降解——这是独一无二的。

transcript.en: RYAN: And the harvesting process is actually good for the trees. There are no downsides to using this source at all.
transcript.cn: 瑞恩：收获过程实际上对树木有益，使用这种来源完全没有缺点。

transcript.en: MADDY: Hemp is another really good sustainable fabric from a natural source.
transcript.cn: 麦迪：麻是另一种非常好的来自天然来源的可持续织物。

transcript.en: RYAN: Yes. Did you know that clothes made from hemp protect the wearer from the sun and it's also antibacterial?
transcript.cn: 瑞恩：是的。您知道由麻制成的衣服可以保护穿戴者免受阳光照射，而且它还具有抗菌性？

transcript.en: MADDY: No, I didn't. But I did read that it's quite hard to grow, so perhaps that's why it's not as common as you'd think.
transcript.cn: 麦迪：不，我不知道。但我确实读到过它很难种植，所以也许这就是为什么它不像您想象的那么普遍。

transcript.en: RYAN: I'm sure that'll change.
transcript.cn: 瑞恩：我相信这会改变的。

groups.title: Invasive species
groups.desc:
Questions 31-40
Complete the notes below.
Write ONE WORD ONLY for each answer.
groups.collect: Invasive species Definition: an animal or plant that causes harm to an environment after being introduced by humans An invasive species can be a problem when it: - eats native species. - introduces a new $22257$ - takes food from native species. - threatens an entire $22258$ How invasive species spread - accidentally e.g. via people returning from their $22259$ or on cargo ships - intentionally e.g. for pest control, or as $22260$ Examples of invasive species Rhinella marina (toads) were introduced to Australia from Hawaii in 1935 to eat a type of insect that was damaging the $22261$ plantations. They failed to solve the problem and became widespread in the north of Australia. They are poisonous for any species that eats them and reduce the food available for native frogs. Japanese knotweed plants were popular among 19th-century gardeners in the UK. Rhododendron plants prevent $22262$ from reaching native plants. Grey squirrels from N. America reduce sources of food for the UK native red squirrels and spread a $22263$ that kills red squirrels. Tackling invasive species Monitoring helps us to understand the $22264$ of invasive species and the impact they have. Setting up a national $22265$ makes it easier to track them. Asking the public to $22266$ and report them helps with monitoring.

questions.analysis: 题干要求填写入侵物种带来的新问题，原文明确指出它们‘may bring a disease with them’，而本地物种对此毫无防御能力。此处‘disease’与题干中的‘introduces a new’形成同义替换，且为不可数名词，符合‘ONE WORD ONLY’的要求。其他如‘pest’或‘insect’虽在文中出现，但不符合该空上下文逻辑。
题干中‘threatens an entire’后需填一个表示整体生态系统的词。原文第11句提到‘putting its survival at risk’，其中‘its’指代前文的‘ecosystem’，因此‘ecosystem’是准确答案。该词精准对应入侵物种对整个生态环境造成的生存威胁，且为单数可数名词，符合语法和语义要求。

题干询问人们无意引入物种的途径之一，原文第13句提到‘people who've been on holiday in another country come back with... seeds... on their clothes’，直接对应‘returning from their holiday’。虽然‘holidays’也可接受，但听力中使用的是单数‘holiday’，更贴近口语表达习惯，且符合‘ONE WORD’要求。
题干要求填写人类有意引入物种的目的之一，与‘pest control’并列。原文第15句明确说‘to use them... or to be pets’，‘pets’作为目的名词，与题干‘as pets’结构完全匹配。尽管‘companions’等词可能语义相近，但原文仅使用‘pets’，且该词简洁准确，符合填空要求。
题干问及甘蔗种植园（sugar plantations）遭害虫破坏，原文第18句提到‘destroying crops of sugar on many of the plantations’，‘crops of sugar’即指甘蔗作物，因此‘sugar’是正确答案。该词直接修饰‘plantations’，符合农业语境，且为不可数名词，满足题目格式要求。

题干描述杜鹃花阻碍某种东西到达本土植物，原文第27句指出它们‘block out so much light that native wild flowers can't grow beneath them’，说明缺乏的是‘light’。‘prevent light from reaching’是对‘block out light’的同义转述，且‘light’为不可数名词，完全符合‘ONE WORD ONLY’的要求。
题干指出灰松鼠传播一种杀死红松鼠的东西，原文第30句明确说‘carry a type of virus that is deadly to the red squirrels’，‘virus’即为所求。虽然‘disease’可能被误选，但原文特指病毒（virus），且‘spread a virus’与‘carry a virus’语义一致，答案更精确。
题干要求填写用于理解入侵物种的某个方面，原文第32句指出‘learning about the behaviour of new species’是控制的第一步，因此‘behaviour’（英式拼写）是正确答案。该词直接出现在关键句中，且与‘understand the... of invasive species’结构吻合，符合学术语境和字数限制。
题干提到建立国家级系统以追踪入侵物种，原文第34句说‘create a database for the whole country’，‘national database’正是对此的概括。‘database’为可数名词单数，准确对应信息共享平台的概念，且是原文原词，无同义替换干扰，答案明确。

题干要求公众采取某项行动以便报告入侵物种，原文第36句明确说‘photograph harmful species... and then to report when and where these were observed’，‘photograph’作为动词原形，与‘report’并列，完全匹配‘asking the public to... and report’的结构，且符合‘ONE WORD ONLY’的要求。

transcript.en: PART 4
You will hear part of an ecology lecture about invasive species of plants and animals.
transcript.cn: 你将听到一段关于动植物入侵物种的生态学讲座。

transcript.en: First, you have some time to look at questions 31 to 40.
transcript.cn: 首先，你有一些时间查看第31至第40题。

transcript.en: Now listen carefully and answer questions 31 to 40.
transcript.cn: 现在请仔细听，并回答第31至第40题。

transcript.en: Today I'm going to talk about invasive species. Let me start by saying what an invasive species is and what it's not.
transcript.cn: 今天我将谈论入侵物种。让我先说说什么是入侵物种，什么不是。

transcript.en: Invasive species are any animal or plant that is introduced into an environment by humans, and which is then harmful to that environment. It's important to be clear that not all introduced species are invasive.
transcript.cn: 入侵物种是指任何由人类引入某一环境的动物或植物，并对该环境造成危害。重要的是要明确，并非所有引入的物种都是入侵性的。

transcript.en: Many introduced – or non-native – species thrive in new areas without posing any threat.
transcript.cn: 许多引入的——或非本土的——物种在新地区茁壮成长而不构成任何威胁。

transcript.en: In some cases, invasive species have changed the natural world beyond recognition, so let's look at the different ways they can be problematic.
transcript.cn: 在某些情况下，入侵物种已经使自然界发生了翻天覆地的变化，所以让我们来看看它们可能造成问题的不同方式。

transcript.en: First of all, invasive species may eat native species, or sometimes they may bring a disease with them, which native species have never faced before and therefore have no defences against.
transcript.cn: 首先，入侵物种可能会吃掉本土物种，或者有时它们可能携带一种本土物种从未面对过的疾病，因此没有防御能力。

transcript.en: Often the invasive species breed very quickly – which further adds to the problem of native species losing their sources of food.
transcript.cn: 通常，入侵物种繁殖非常迅速——这进一步加剧了本土物种失去食物来源的问题。

transcript.en: Species invasions are one of the biggest causes of damage in an ecosystem, actually putting its survival at risk.
transcript.cn: 物种入侵是生态系统受损的最大原因之一，实际上使其生存面临风险。

transcript.en: So, how do invasive species spread? Without a doubt, the biggest cause is human activity.
transcript.cn: 那么，入侵物种是如何传播的？毫无疑问，最大的原因是人类活动。

transcript.en: This could be intentional, or it could be accidental, such as when people who've been on holiday in another country come back with, say, the seeds of plants on their clothes or shoes.
transcript.cn: 这可能是有意的，也可能是无意的，比如当人们在另一个国家度假后回来时，衣服或鞋子上带着植物种子。

transcript.en: Plants and animals, especially insects, arrive in or on the cargo of ships, and then escape into their new 'home'.
transcript.cn: 植物和动物，尤其是昆虫，藏在或附在船舶货物中到达，然后逃入它们的新「家」。

transcript.en: But sometimes humans deliberately move animals and plants around the world, for example to use them to control pests on farms, or to be pets.
transcript.cn: 但有时人类故意在世界各地移动动植物，例如用它们来控制农场上的害虫，或作为宠物。

transcript.en: This can go very wrong if those animals and plants move into wild settings and start breeding or begin growing in ways that weren't predicted.
transcript.cn: 如果这些动植物进入野外环境并开始以未预料到的方式繁殖或生长，这可能会出现严重问题。

transcript.en: Let's now look at an example of an invasive species here in Australia: Rhinella marina is a species of toad that was deliberately introduced from Hawaii in 1935 as a form of biological control.
transcript.cn: 现在让我们看看澳大利亚的一个入侵物种例子：海蟾蜍是一种蟾蜍物种，1935年从夏威夷故意引入作为生物防治手段。

transcript.en: It was hoped that the toads would eat the grey-backed beetles responsible for destroying crops of sugar on many of the plantations.
transcript.cn: 人们希望蟾蜍能够吃掉负责毁坏许多种植园甘蔗作物的灰背甲虫。

transcript.en: At first, just a handful of toads were released by scientists into Queensland, but this number soon grew as other states followed suit.
transcript.cn: 起初，科学家们只在昆士兰州放生了少量蟾蜍，但随着其他州纷纷效仿，这个数字很快增加了。

transcript.en: Within two years, 62,000 young toads had been released into the wild.
transcript.cn: 两年内，62,000只幼年蟾蜍被放生到野外。

transcript.en: The toads did nothing to protect the plantations, but they did reproduce rapidly and could soon be found all over the northern half of the country.
transcript.cn: 蟾蜍没有起到保护种植园的作用，但它们确实快速繁殖，很快遍布全国北半部。

transcript.en: The toads are poisonous at every stage of their life cycle, and anything that eats them will die.
transcript.cn: 蟾蜍在其生命周期的每个阶段都有毒，任何吃了它们的东西都会死亡。

transcript.en: My second example regarding invasive species is the United Kingdom. Actually, there are more than three-thousand invasive species there, including some that are extremely common.
transcript.cn: 关于入侵物种的第二个例子是英国。实际上，那里有超过三千种入侵物种，其中一些极为常见。

transcript.en: Some invasive plants, such as Japanese knotweed, have had a devastating impact on parts of the UK.
transcript.cn: 一些入侵植物，如虎杖，对英国部分地区产生了毁灭性影响。

transcript.en: Gardeners in the nineteenth century considered it a beautiful ornamental plant – which it is, when it's kept under control – but it soon spread into the countryside and remains a problem even to this day as it's so hard to eradicate.
transcript.cn: 19世纪的园丁认为它是一种美丽的观赏植物——当然，在受控时它确实是——但它很快蔓延到农村，直到今天仍然是一个问题，因为它很难根除。

transcript.en: Another invasive plant is rhododendrons, which can be found in UK parks and woodlands.
transcript.cn: 另一种入侵植物是杜鹃花，可以在英国的公园和林地中找到。

transcript.en: Their introduction dates back to 1763, but they're now seen as harmful because they block out so much light that native wild flowers can't grow beneath them.
transcript.cn: 它们的引入可以追溯到1763年，但现在被视为有害的，因为它们遮挡了太多阳光，使得本土野花无法在其下面生长。

transcript.en: And then there are grey squirrels, which are one invasive species almost everyone in the UK will have come across.
transcript.cn: 然后是灰松鼠，这是英国几乎每个人都会遇到的一种入侵物种。

transcript.en: They were brought to the UK from North America and introduced to private estates around the 1870s but are now found everywhere, from forests to city squares.
transcript.cn: 它们从北美被带到英国，大约在1870年代被引入私人庄园，但现在到处都有，从森林到城市广场。

transcript.en: Grey squirrels have outcompeted the smaller, native red squirrels. They both eat the same food, and the grey squirrels carry a type of virus that is deadly to the red squirrels.
transcript.cn: 灰松鼠已经超过了较小的本土红松鼠。它们都吃同样的食物，而灰松鼠携带一种对红松鼠致命的病毒。

transcript.en: Red squirrel populations have collapsed, and there are only a handful of sites left in the UK where they're found.
transcript.cn: 红松鼠种群已经崩溃，英国只有少数几个地方还能找到它们。

transcript.en: An important question for ecologists worldwide is, what can we do to tackle the problem of invasive species? The first step in controlling invasive species is learning about the behaviour of new species coming into the country.
transcript.cn: 全球生态学家面临的一个重要问题是，我们能做什么来解决入侵物种的问题？控制入侵物种的第一步是了解进入该国的新物种的行为。

transcript.en: Monitoring is an important part of this, so that we can know if the new species begins to have a negative impact in its new environment.
transcript.cn: 监测是其中的重要部分，这样我们就能知道新物种是否开始对其新环境产生负面影响。

transcript.en: One effective way to keep track of invasive species is to create a database for the whole country.
transcript.cn: 追踪入侵物种的一种有效方法是为整个国家建立一个数据库。

transcript.en: That way, all relevant authorities and agencies can share important information and take whatever action's needed. But the public also have a vital role to play in this process.
transcript.cn: 这样，所有相关当局和机构可以共享重要信息并采取所需的任何行动，但公众在这一过程中也有重要的作用。

transcript.en: They should be encouraged to photograph harmful species – because this helps with identification – and then to report when and where these were observed.
transcript.cn: 应该鼓励他们拍摄有害物种的照片——因为这有助于识别——然后报告观察到这些物种的时间和地点。

transcript.en: But it's important to tell people not to destroy or even touch what they've found.
transcript.cn: 但重要的是告诉人们不要破坏甚至触摸他们发现的东西。

transcript.en: Now, I'm going to move on to …
transcript.cn: 现在，我将继续……
